headline: Survey at 36% completion; strong regional concentration masks coverage gaps.
collection_status: 481 of 1,350 responses collected (35.6% of target). Lindi, Mara, and Tanga account for 57% of all data. Collection pace suggests need for accelerated outreach in underrepresented regions (Rukwa at 19 responses, Singida at 28).
key_findings: Teacher respondents dominate (65% of sample): 195 KF lower-primary, 115 head teachers, 65 KF upper-primary. District/regional officials severely underrepresented (16 responses across DEO, REO, ARC, REC roles).
Expansion appetite is clear: 175 respondents prioritize KF expansion; 116 want new programs. Only 9 cite research—policy and implementation readiness drive agenda.
Satisfaction-readiness gap: Partner satisfaction high (4.4/5) but scale readiness low (2.8/5). Stakeholders confident in current partnerships but doubt operational capacity to expand.
Qualitative themes align with quantitative priorities: feedback emphasizes sustained teacher incentives, multi-region rollout, government policy integration, and digital monitoring systems.
flags: Rukwa (19) and Singida (28) critically undersampled; risk of regional bias in findings and representativeness.
Official stakeholder participation weak: only 16 district/regional education officers across all regions; policy validation and buy-in may be understated.
Scale readiness (2.8/5) contradicts expansion appetite; risk of over-committing before operational foundations strengthened.
recommendation: Prioritize 10-day surge collection in Rukwa, Singida, Songwe, and Mtwara to reach 60% coverage. Simultaneously conduct targeted outreach to DEO/REO offices to capture official policy perspective—essential before advancing government integration agenda.
